# Jiakai Gu's Blog

> `Jiakai Gu's Blog` is a personal technology blog focusing on sharing tech and personal experiences.

## Main Sections

- [Home Page](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/): Home Page
- [Archive](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/archive/): Archive Page
- [Tags](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/tags/): Tags Page
- [Til](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/til/): Til Page
- [About](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/about/): About Page

## Content

- [Trying Out Perplexity Max](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2026/06/trying-perplexity-max/): A one-month hands-on review of Perplexity Max, including Model Council, Computer, research tools, and mobile use.
- [June Is Still Young, Back to Writing](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2026/06/june-back-to-writing/): Returns to monthly blogging after securing a vocational-college teaching job, reflecting on civil-service interviews, the national exam, and life changes.
- [Reflections on Claude Max](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2026/01/claude-max-aftertaste/): Reassesses Claude Max 5x during a thesis deadline, focusing on Opus 4.5 in Claude Code, quota economics, and the work it enabled.
- [When LLMs Start Drawing Answers](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2025/12/when-llms-start-drawing-answers/): Compares how ChatGPT, Grok, Yuanbao, Google AI Mode, Phind, Metaso, and GLM turn text answers into useful visual or interactive explanations.
- [From Trial to Official: My Relationship Journey](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2025/11/from-trial-to-official/): A personal record of moving from an online acquaintance to an official relationship, culminating in 52 roses and a first kiss in November 2025.
- [Claude Sonnet 4.5 Review](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2025/09/claude-sonnet-4-5-review/): Reviews Sonnet 4.5 for vibe coding, writing, Imagine with Claude, and Pro quotas, while weighing capability gains against Anthropic's tighter limits.
- [Claude Max Preview](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2025/09/claude-max-preview/): Tests a Claude Max subscription and Claude for Chrome on live browsing and research tasks, including its automation strengths, limits, and account risk.
- [Make Claude Great Again!](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2025/08/make-claude-great-again/): Explains how Opus 4.1 and Claude Code restored confidence in Claude for writing and coding after disappointing Opus 4 outputs.
- [Manus Experience](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2025/07/manus-experience/): Tests free and Basic Manus agents: paid mode improves effort and PPTs, but weak source judgment, opaque models, and bot checks undermine reliability.
- [Grok 4 Real Experience](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2025/07/grok-4-real-experience/): Tests Grok 4's X-native search, agent workflow, low censorship, image recognition, Deep Research, and weaker Chinese-language performance.
- [Claude 4 Experience](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2025/05/claude-4-experience/): Evaluates Claude 4 Opus and Sonnet across coding, writing, API access, and Claude Code, then revisits the product after Max-plan use.
- [Google I/O 2025 Notes](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2025/05/google-io-2025-notes/): Organizes Google I/O 2025's AI announcements across Gemini, AI Mode, Flow, Veo, Stitch, Chrome, Project Astra, Android XR, and live translation.
- [ChatGPT o3, o4 mini Experience](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2025/04/chatgpt-o3-o4-mini-experience/): Compares ChatGPT o3 and o4-mini through coding, search, image understanding, hallucination, limits, and everyday use after launch.
- [Self-Hosted Kasm Workspaces](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2025/03/self-hosted-kasm-workspaces/): Deploys Kasm Workspaces for isolated browser sessions, covering persistent profiles, clipboard behavior, resource cleanup, and use with datacenter IPs.
- [Gemini 2.5 Pro Experience](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2025/03/gemini-2-5-pro-experience/): A frequently updated evaluation of Gemini 2.5 Pro's reasoning, coding, OCR, long context, API behavior, and student access as the model evolved.
- [Phind VS Devv](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2025/03/phind-vs-devv/): Compares Phind and Devv after a month of paid use, covering price, model access, search quality, limits, and why only Devv was worth renewing.
- [Claude 3.7 Sonnet Experience](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2025/02/claude-3-7-sonnet-experience/): Tracks Claude 3.7 Sonnet from its hybrid-reasoning launch to Claude Code's rise, with tests of coding, web search, Chinese writing, and quota limits.
- [Gemini 2.0 Experience](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2025/02/gemini-2-0-experience/): Follows Gemini 2.0 across the app and API, testing Deep Research, Canvas, Audio Overview, Flash and Pro models, and its uneven product rollout.
- [Songs Doubao Real-Time Voice Can Sing On-Pitch](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2025/01/doubao-real-time-on-pitch-songs/): Collects 13 songs that Doubao's real-time voice can sing on pitch, alongside cutesy voice and imitation demos, and compares it with GLM Realtime.
- [GLM Realtime API Experience](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2025/01/glm-realtime-api-experience/): Builds a Claude-generated GLM Realtime API demo, tests conversation and its seven-song limit, and compares it with competing voice models.
- [OpenAI December Release Notes](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2024/12/notes-about-openai-december-updates/): A day-by-day assessment of OpenAI's 12 Days releases, from o1 Pro and Sora to Canvas, Projects, Advanced Voice, and o3.
- [Claude Account Suspension Resolution](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2024/11/claude-account-suspension-resolution/): Recounts a Claude account suspension and the eventual workaround: using a clean remote Windows environment and cautious network routing.
- [Claude 3.5 Haiku Review](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2024/11/claude-3-5-haiku-review/): Benchmarks Claude 3.5 Haiku's speed, pricing, July 2024 knowledge cutoff, coding ability, and lack of image input against Sonnet.
- [Gemini Advanced October 2024 Review](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2024/11/gemini-advanced-2024-october-review/): A multi-month Gemini Advanced review covering YouTube summaries, API limits, image generation, Gems, Deep Research, and the later 2.5 Pro upgrade.
- [Anthropic Updates Evaluation](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2024/10/anthropic-updates-evaluation/): Tests Claude's October 2024 Computer Use and revised 3.5 Sonnet, noting stronger coding and reasoning alongside prompt-sensitive Chinese output.
- [Stay Away from GreenCloud, Soul Ascending](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2024/10/stay-away-from-greencloud/): Details the GreenCloud San Jose ransomware outage, unrecoverable VPS data loss, rescue attempts, and a successful $100 PayPal refund.
- [bolt.new First Experience](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2024/10/bolt-new-first-experience/): Builds a live Markdown tree generator with bolt.new, then evaluates its package support, token use, Netlify deployment, and backend limitations.
- [Self-Hosted Bookmark App—Karakeep (formerly Hoarder)](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2024/10/self-hosted-bookmark-app-hoarder/): Migrates from an expired Raindrop subscription to self-hosted Karakeep, covering Docker deployment, AI tagging, mobile access, and the rename from Hoarder.
- [ChatGPT 4o with Canvas Review](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2024/10/chatgpt-4o-canvas-review/): Tests ChatGPT 4o Canvas shortcuts for drafting and code, including inline edits, reading-level changes, emoji passes, and rendered previews.
- [Xiaohongshu RSS Solutions](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2024/09/xiaohongshu-rss-tips/): Maintains a Xiaohongshu RSS setup with cookies, cache limits, and anti-crawl safeguards, while warning that full-text scraping triggers captchas.
- [Why CSDN Is a Pile of Shit](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2024/09/why-is-csdn-a-pile-of-shit/): Explaining why CSDN is a pile of shit, targeting the platform, not creators using it.
- [How I Found a Bargain Bero Host VPS](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2024/08/unexpected-bero-host-vps-bargain/): How a chance encounter led to a discounted Bero Host VPS, including its configuration, network, price, and early performance.
- [Meta.AI US IP Region Not Supported Solutions](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2024/08/meta-ai-us-ip-restriction-solutions/): Shows why a US IP alone did not bypass Meta.AI's region check and how a newly registered Facebook account on a native US connection restored access.
- [Proton Family Plan First Taste](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2024/08/proton-family-plan-first-taste/): Reviews a $48-per-year Proton Family seat across VPN, Mail, Pass, Drive, Calendar, and Wallet, including connectivity and feature limitations.
- [Self-Host Pixelfed as an Instagram Alternative](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2024/07/build-your-own-xiaohongshu-instagram-alternative-pixelfed/): Deploy Pixelfed with Docker Compose, configure its domain and mail settings, and run a federated photo-sharing service.
- [A Young Person's First ASN](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2024/06/the-first-asn-of-young-people/): Obtains AS214948 and an IPv6 /48, configures a BGP session with bird2, then documents rising costs and the eventual return of the resources.
- [Self-Host Zoraxy: An Alternative to Nginx Proxy Manager](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2024/05/selfhost-zoraxy-an-alternative-to-nginx-proxy-manager/): A practical Docker guide to Zoraxy reverse proxies, TLS certificates, access control, and container networking.
- [Alternatives to Google One VPN Shutdown](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2024/04/alternatives-to-google-one-vpn-shutdown/): Tracks post-shutdown alternatives to Google One VPN—from WARP and dual-ISP VPS routes to residential IPs—and the tradeoffs in cost and reliability.
- [The Fake Self-Hosting Enthusiast](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2024/04/fake-selfhost-lover/): Why more than 50 self-hosted services gathered dust: weak personal need, ongoing maintenance, and SaaS products that worked better.
- [Experience Sharing on Ikihost IP Change Ordeal](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2024/03/experience-sharing-on-ikihost-ip-change-ordeal/): Reconstructs an Ikihost VPS after an IP-change maintenance failure, using rescue mode to recover files before rebuilding the server.
- [Solution for Gemini Android App 'Device Not Supported'](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2024/02/solving-gemini-device-compatibility-issues/): Gets the Gemini Android app working on an unsupported Xiaomi phone by installing and initializing Google Assistant and the Google app.
- [Lightsail Migration to IPv6-only Instance Process Record](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2024/01/migrating-to-ipv6-only-instance-on-lightsail/): Migrates an AWS Lightsail server to the unchanged $3.50 IPv6-only plan, covering snapshots, SSH access, Cloudflare DNS, and IPv4 reachability.
- [Solution for apt Install Error 'Operation not permitted'](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2024/01/fix-apt-install-operation-not-permitted-error/): Traces apt's 'Operation not permitted' error to BaoTa System Hardening making /usr/bin read-only, then restores writes by disabling the rule.
- [ChatGPT Team Account Experience](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2024/01/chatgpt-team-experience/): Explains the early ChatGPT Team workspace, its higher message limits and privacy controls, while warning about an invitation loophole used to join.
- [2023 Black Friday Server Shopping Summary](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/11/summary-of-server-shop-of-2023-bf/): Details 2023 Black Friday server purchases from ColoCrossing, RackNerd, CloudCone, and others, including prices, routes, and which deals were kept.
- [My App Defaults 2023](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/11/my-app-defaults-2023/): The author's 2023 daily stack for email, notes, cloud storage, browsers, communication, media, development, and AI.
- [Fix Ubuntu 'Unable to Mount Root FS' on RackNerd](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/11/racknerd-ubuntu-ufs-fix/): Recover a RackNerd Ubuntu VPS from 'UFS: unable to mount root fs on unknown-block' by booting an older kernel and restoring networking.
- [Fix ChatGPT Android 'Disallowed ISP' Errors](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/10/chatgpt-android-error-disallowed-isp-solution/): Diagnose ChatGPT Android's 'Disallowed ISP' and 'There is a problem with your request' errors with endpoint checks and a secondary proxy exit.
- [Self-hosting Proxigram with Docker](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/10/use-docker-compose-to-build-proxigram/): Deploy Proxigram and Redis with Docker Compose, enable RSS, configure media proxying, and expose the privacy-friendly Instagram frontend.
- [Solution for HXServers Network Issues](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/10/solving-hxservers-network-issues/): Restore networking after converting an HXServers VPS to Windows by entering its IP, subnet mask, and gateway through VNC.
- [Experiencing iCloud+ Private Relay](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/10/experience-icloud-plus-private-relay/): Enable iCloud+ Private Relay on a Mac in China by routing it through Clash for Windows TUN mode and a Windows hotspot.
- [HXServers Google One VPN Node Setup Guide](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/10/hxservers-google-one-node-setup-guide/): Configure an HXServers Windows VPS as a Google One VPN node, including the VirtFusion networking workaround and later provider shutdown.
- [Fix Conda UnicodeDecodeError in Windows Git Bash](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/09/conda-activation-error-unicode-decode-gbk/): How to resolve the 'gbk codec can't decode byte' activation failure by correcting Conda's encoding behavior in Windows Git Bash.
- [Notes on Migrating from One BandwagonHost Machine to Another](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/09/notes-about-migrating-from-one-bandwagon-host-to-another/): Migrate a BandwagonHost VPS with snapshots, then update SSH, Cloudflare DNS, monitoring agents, and other machine-specific settings.
- [Use Windows Hotspot and Clash TUN with Kindle](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/09/using-windows-hotspot-and-clash-tun-for-kindle-internet-access/): Configure a Windows hotspot and Clash TUN mode so a Kindle can route its traffic through the computer's proxy.
- [Fix Git Push 'Did Not Receive Expected Object'](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/09/fix-git-push-fatal-error-did-not-receive-expected-object/): How to recover when git push fails with 'remote: fatal: did not receive expected object' after repository object corruption.
- [Set IP Geolocation to North Korea or Antarctica](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/08/methods-for-setting-ip-to-north-korea-and-antarctica/): How custom geofeed data and BGP announcements can make an IP prefix appear in North Korea or Antarctica in geolocation databases.
- [Solution for ChatGPT Android Date and Time Error](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/08/solving-date-and-time-error-in-chatgpt-android-app/): Fix ChatGPT Android's date-and-time warning by checking the proxy, Google Play components, automatic time settings, or clearing app data.
- [Fix Caddy Startup on BandwagonHost The Plan V2](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/06/solving-caddy-failure-on-bandwagonhost-theplanv2/): How to correct Caddy's startup failure on BandwagonHost The Plan V2 by adjusting its service configuration.
- [Solution for Chika Launcher Crashing on Windows Server](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/06/fix-chika-crash-on-windows-server/): Stop Chika Launcher from crashing on Windows Server by changing Data Execution Prevention settings after moving game automation to a VPS.
- [ChatGPT Plugins List](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/06/chatgpt-plugins-list/): A curated snapshot of roughly 250 ChatGPT plugins available in June 2023, organized to help Plus users find useful tools.
- [Unlocking ChatGPT for VPS in Non-OpenAI Service Regions](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/06/unlock-chatgpt-when-vps-been-judged-cn-by-cloudflare/): Route ChatGPT traffic through a secondary proxy when WARP leaves a Hong Kong VPS—or another misclassified IP—outside supported regions.
- [Reflections on OpenAI Banning Plus Users](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/05/thinking-of-openai-ban-plus-customers-in-527/): Examines the May 2023 Plus account purge, likely risk factors, refund options, and the author's decision to rebuild with a safer account.
- [Feed43's Successor—RSSEverything RSS Feed Creation Tutorial](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/03/create-rss-feed-with-rsseverything-tutorial/): Build a custom RSS feed with RSSEverything by defining CSS selectors, previewing extracted items, and publishing the generated feed URL.
- [Fix New Bing Network Errors with WARP](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/03/solutions-for-network-error-of-new-bing/): How WARP restores access when New Bing reports, 'Sorry, looks like your network settings are preventing access to this feature.'
- [Hugo Theme Development Diary](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/03/hugo-theme-development-diary/): A template-by-template diary of building this blog's Hugo theme, covering base layouts, sidebars, single pages, taxonomies, lists, and RSS.
- [Solution for Google Voice Stuck on 'Connecting'](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/03/the-solution-of-google-voice-is-connecting/): Finds that a WebRTC-blocking Chrome extension caused Google Voice's 'Connecting' loop; disabling it restored browser calls.
- [Are Docker Container Services Not Limited by Firewall?](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/03/will-docker-container-be-limited-by-firewall/): Solutions for Docker container services bypassing firewall restrictions.
- [RSS-Bridge Setup Guide](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/02/use-docker-compose-to-build-rssbridge/): Deploy RSS-Bridge with Docker, enable the Picuki Instagram bridge, set the timezone, and publish it behind Caddy.
- [Ways to Subscribe to Instagram via RSS](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/01/use-rss-to-subscribe-instagram/): Compares RSS-Bridge, Feedbro, Telegram bots, Proxigram, and other ways to follow Instagram, with updates on routes that later stopped working.
- [Fix Nginx 'Failed to Parse PID' After Installation](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/01/a-solution-about-nginx-problem/): How to resolve the 'Failed to parse PID from file /usr/local/nginx/logs/nginx.pid: Invalid argument' service error after installing Nginx.
- [The Execution Process of 'ls > list' Command](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/01/the-execution-process-ls-list/): Explains why the shell creates or truncates the output file before running ls, causing the new list file to appear in its own redirected output.
- [Read Later = Never Read](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2023/01/read-later-equals-to-not-read/): Why a 2,500-item Pocket backlog turned read-later into information hoarding, and the rules adopted after clearing it.
- [Removing Trailing Slashes from Hugo Blog URLs](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2022/11/hugo-remove-trailing-slash/): Uses HUGO_UGLYURLS on Windows and Netlify when uglyURLs alone fails, then revisits canonical duplication that hurt Google indexing.
- [Fix SSH Proxy 'Connection Closed by Foreign Host'](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2022/10/ssh-connect-by-proxy-error-connection-closed-by-foreign-host-solution/): A two-hour troubleshooting account of fixing SSH proxy sessions that ended with 'Connection closed by foreign host.'
- [Why JavaScript Statements Need Semicolons](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2022/09/you-should-add-a-semicolon-when-a-statement-end-in-js/): What a newsletter's JavaScript snippets revealed about safer statement endings and the value of explicit semicolons.
- [Installing code-server Using a One-Click Script](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2022/08/use-script-to-install-code-server/): Install code-server using a one-click script, improving upon the rough installation process shown in my Bilibili video.
- [My GitHub Student Developer Pack Application Journey](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2022/06/github-education-developer-kit-application-journey/): After being instantly rejected 49 times by GitHub's automated system, I finally succeeded on the 50th attempt.
- [The March 24th Incident - Gitee's Predicament](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2022/03/324-gitee-affair/): On the afternoon of March 24, 2022, Gitee banned all image hosting repositories. Here are my thoughts on this matter.
- [Creating Animated SVG Effects on Your GitHub Profile](https://blog.gujiakai.top/en/2022/03/github-svg/): This article explains how to create animated SVG effects on your GitHub profile page.
